title: narcissus nobilis
intro: Narcissus nobilis, also known as the wild daffodil or trumpet daffodil, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. Traditionally, the bulbs of Narcissus nobilis have been used for their medicinal properties, containing compounds like galantamine, which have been shown to have potential in treating conditions such as Alzheimer's disease and other cognitive impairments. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off bacteria, and acting as a natural sedative. In herbal preparations, the bulbs of Narcissus nobilis are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although proper dosing and caution should be exercised due to potential side effects.
